Title: Abandoned cart detection firing false positives
Description: Massive spike in 'abandoned cart detected' audit log entries. Carts updated just 30 minutes ago are being flagged as abandoned. Customer support received complaints about premature 'come back to your cart' emails. DataDog logs show abandoned cart detection count jumped from ~5/run to 200+/run. DB audit_log entries show lastUpdated timestamps only minutes old.

Title: Order totals missing tax — customers undercharged 8%
Description: Finance reconciliation flagged that order totals are consistently lower than expected. The stored total on orders does not include tax_amount even though tax is calculated and stored correctly in its own column. Every order is undercharged by exactly 8%. Revenue leak and tax compliance issue. Verify: SELECT id, subtotal, tax_amount, shipping_cost, total, (subtotal + tax_amount + shipping_cost) as expected FROM Order.

Title: Admin dashboard revenue inflated by refunded orders
Description: Admin dashboard total revenue figure includes refunded orders in the calculation. Leadership seeing inaccurate revenue numbers. DataDog revenue metric is consistently higher than payment processor net revenue. Compare dashboard total against DB query excluding refunded orders to confirm discrepancy.

Title: Coupons with minimum order threshold applied to small carts
Description: Coupons with minimum_order requirements (e.g. FLAT20 requires $100 min) are being accepted on carts well below the threshold. Validation appears to compare cart total against the discount_value instead of minimum_order. Marketing reports FLAT20 redemption rate is 4x higher than projected. Revenue loss from discounts applied to ineligible orders.

Title: Guest shopping carts deleted prematurely by cron job
Description: Guest users report carts randomly emptied. Cron job that should clean up guest carts older than 7 days is deleting ALL guest carts including ones created minutes ago. DataDog logs show unusually high cleanup counts. DB Cart table has 0 guest rows after every cron run. Directly impacts conversion rates for non-logged-in shoppers.
